name: TealHQ
description: Job search tracker — clients build resumes and track applications here
review: This is the tool I recommend to every single client on day one. TealHQ lets you track applications, tailor your resume to each job posting, and keep your entire job search organized in one place. If you're applying to more than 5 jobs, you need this.

name: Simplify.jobs
description: AI job application tool — autofills applications across job boards
review: Simplify saves you hours every week by autofilling job applications. It pulls your info and drops it into application forms across multiple job boards. It's not perfect for every site, but the time savings are real. A game changer for the application grind.

name: Lavender
description: AI email writing assistant for outreach and follow-ups
review: Lavender coaches you on your emails in real time. It scores your message and tells you exactly what to fix: too long, too formal, missing personalization. I use it with clients for networking outreach and follow-up emails. Your reply rates will go up.

name: Yoodli
description: AI speech and interview practice tool with real-time feedback
review: Yoodli records your practice interviews and gives you feedback on filler words, pacing, and confidence. It's like having a speaking coach available 24/7. I recommend this to every client who gets nervous in interviews. Practice out loud, not in your head.

name: Uli AI
description: Free AI mock interview tool with instant feedback
review: Uli is a free AI mock interview tool that I recommend at every workshop I run. You pick the role, it asks you questions, and gives you feedback on your answers. It's not the same as practicing with a real person, but it's the next best thing. And it's free.

name: Boardy AI
description: AI networking tool — connects professionals based on goals and interests
review: Boardy uses AI to match you with people who can actually help your career. It's like a networking event that runs in the background. I've seen clients make real connections through it. If cold outreach feels awkward, this takes the pressure off.

name: Hunter.io
description: Email finder — locate hiring manager and recruiter contact info
review: Hunter.io finds email addresses for people at any company. When you want to reach out to a hiring manager directly instead of applying through the black hole of an online portal, this is how you find their email. 25 free searches per month.

name: Glassdoor
description: Company reviews and salary data from real employees
review: Glassdoor is your first stop for salary research and company culture intel. Look at the trends, not individual reviews. If 30 people mention bad management, that's a pattern. I teach clients to bring Glassdoor data into interviews as smart, researched questions.

name: Indeed
description: Job search engine with salary data and company reviews
review: Indeed is solid for salary research and finding job postings. I don't recommend relying only on job boards (you'll end up in the hamster wheel), but Indeed's salary data is useful when you're building your negotiation range.

name: Payscale
description: Salary data and compensation reports by role, location, and experience
review: Payscale gives you detailed salary ranges by role, location, and experience level. When a recruiter asks your salary expectations, you want three data points from three different sources. Payscale is always one of them.

name: Levels.fyi
description: Tech compensation data — real salary reports from employees at tech companies
review: If you're in tech, Levels.fyi is gold. Real compensation data from real employees at real companies. Base salary, stock, bonuses, the full picture. Way more accurate than Glassdoor for tech roles. I use this with every tech client.

name: Crunchbase
description: Startup and company data: funding rounds, company size, leadership
review: Crunchbase tells you how much funding a company has raised, who their investors are, and how fast they're growing. If you're considering a startup, this helps you figure out if they can actually afford to pay you. Do your homework.

name: Fathom
description: AI meeting recorder — transcribes calls and generates action items
review: I use Fathom for every single coaching call. It records, transcribes, and pulls out action items automatically. Over 353 calls recorded and counting. If you're doing informational interviews or networking calls, record them (with permission) so you can follow up on specific details later.

name: Zoom
description: Video conferencing for interviews, coaching sessions, and networking calls
review: Zoom is still the standard for video calls. Most interviews and networking conversations happen here. Make sure your audio is clean, your background isn't distracting, and your camera is at eye level. The basics matter more than you think.

name: Calendly
description: Scheduling tool — book calls without the back-and-forth emails
review: Calendly eliminates the 'when are you free?' email chain. I use it for all my discovery calls and coaching sessions. If you're doing a lot of networking, set up a free Calendly link and drop it in your outreach messages. Makes you look professional.

name: Skool
description: Community platform — home of the ClearCareer community (171+ members)
review: Skool is where our ClearCareer community lives. 171+ members, top 5% on the platform. It's where clients support each other, share wins, do accountability check-ins, and get access to resources you won't find anywhere else. The community is the secret weapon.
